    1528             : 
    1529             : // Reads directly bytes from the file using ReadMultiRange(), and by-pass
    1530             : // block reading. Restricted to simple TIFF configurations
    1531             : // (uncompressed data, standard data types). Particularly useful to extract

    4890             : /************************************************************************/
    4891             : /*                       FillCacheForOtherBands()                       */
    4892             : /************************************************************************/
    4893             : 
    4894      148819 : CPLErr GTiffRasterBand::FillCacheForOtherBands( int nBlockXOff, int nBlockYOff )
    4895             : 
    4896             : {
    4897             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    4898             : /*      In the fairly common case of pixel interleaved 8bit data        */
    4899             : /*      that is multi-band, lets push the rest of the data into the     */
    4900             : /*      block cache too, to avoid (hopefully) having to redecode it.    */
    4901             : /*                                                                      */
    4902             : /*      Our following logic actually depends on the fact that the       */
    4903             : /*      this block is already loaded, so subsequent calls will end      */
    4904             : /*      up back in this method and pull from the loaded block.          */
    4905             : /*                                                                      */
    4906             : /*      Be careful not entering this portion of code from               */
    4907             : /*      the other bands, otherwise we'll get very deep nested calls     */
    4908             : /*      and O(nBands^2) performance !                                   */
    4909             : /*                                                                      */
    4910             : /*      If there are many bands and the block cache size is not big     */
    4911             : /*      enough to accommodate the size of all the blocks, don't enter   */
    4912             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    4913      148819 :     CPLErr eErr = CE_None;
    4914      446449 :     if( m_poGDS->nBands != 1 &&
    4915      297623 :         m_poGDS->nBands < 128 && // avoid caching for datasets with too many bands
    4916      343569 :         !m_poGDS->m_bLoadingOtherBands &&
    4917       45936 :         static_cast<GPtrDiff_t>(nBlockXSize) * nBlockYSize * GDALGetDataTypeSizeBytes(eDataType) <
    4918       45936 :         GDALGetCacheMax64() / m_poGDS->nBands )
    4919             :     {
    4920       36892 :         m_poGDS->m_bLoadingOtherBands = true;
    4921             : 
    4922      176187 :         for( int iOtherBand = 1; iOtherBand <= m_poGDS->nBands; ++iOtherBand )
    4923             :         {
    4924      139298 :             if( iOtherBand == nBand )
    4925       36889 :                 continue;
    4926             : 
    4927             :             GDALRasterBlock *poBlock = m_poGDS->GetRasterBand(iOtherBand)->
    4928      102409 :                 GetLockedBlockRef(nBlockXOff,nBlockYOff);
    4929      102399 :             if( poBlock == nullptr )
    4930             :             {
    4931           0 :                 eErr = CE_Failure;
    4932           0 :                 break;
    4933             :             }
    4934      102399 :             poBlock->DropLock();
    4935             :         }
    4936             : 
    4937       36889 :         m_poGDS->m_bLoadingOtherBands = false;
    4938             :     }
    4939             : 
    4940      148817 :     return eErr;
    4941             : }

    4993             :

    5047             : 
    5048             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    5049             : /*      On write of pixel interleaved data, we might as well flush      */

    6459             : /************************************************************************/
    6460             : /*                            FloatToHalf()                             */
    6461             : /************************************************************************/
    6462             : 
    6463      241232 : static GUInt16 FloatToHalf( GUInt32 iFloat32, bool& bHasWarned )
    6464             : {
    6465      241232 :     GUInt32 iSign =     (iFloat32 >> 31) & 0x00000001;
    6466      241232 :     GUInt32 iExponent = (iFloat32 >> 23) & 0x000000ff;
    6467      241232 :     GUInt32 iMantissa = iFloat32         & 0x007fffff;
    6468             : 
    6469      241232 :     if (iExponent == 255)
    6470             :     {
    6471          10 :         if (iMantissa == 0)
    6472             :         {
    6473             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6474             : /*       Positive or negative infinity.                                 */
    6475             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6476             : 
    6477           4 :             return static_cast<GUInt16>((iSign << 15) | 0x7C00);
    6478             :         }
    6479             :         else
    6480             :         {
    6481             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6482             : /*       NaN -- preserve sign and significand bits.                     */
    6483             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6484           6 :             if( iMantissa >> 13 )
    6485             :                 return static_cast<GUInt16>((iSign << 15) | 0x7C00 |
    6486           4 :                                                             (iMantissa >> 13));
    6487             : 
    6488           2 :             return static_cast<GUInt16>((iSign << 15) | 0x7E00);
    6489             :         }
    6490             :     }
    6491             : 
    6492      241222 :     if( iExponent <= 127 - 15 )
    6493             :     {
    6494             :         // Zero, float32 denormalized number or float32 too small normalized
    6495             :         // number
    6496        1502 :         if( 13 + 1 + 127 - 15 - iExponent >= 32 )
    6497        1497 :             return static_cast<GUInt16>(iSign << 15);
    6498             : 
    6499             :         // Return a denormalized number
    6500             :         return static_cast<GUInt16>((iSign << 15) |
    6501           5 :                 ((iMantissa | 0x00800000) >> (13 + 1 + 127 - 15 - iExponent)));
    6502             :     }
    6503      239720 :     if( iExponent - (127 - 15) >= 31 )
    6504             :     {
    6505           1 :         if( !bHasWarned )
    6506             :         {
    6507           1 :             bHasWarned = true;
    6508           1 :             float fVal = 0.0f;
    6509           1 :             memcpy(&fVal, &iFloat32, 4);
    6510             :             CPLError(
    6511             :                 CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    6512             :                 "Value %.8g is beyond range of float16. Converted to %sinf",
    6513           1 :                 fVal, (fVal > 0) ? "+" : "-");
    6514             :         }
    6515           1 :         return static_cast<GUInt16>((iSign << 15) | 0x7C00);  // Infinity
    6516             :     }
    6517             : 
    6518             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6519             : /*       Normalized number.                                             */
    6520             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6521             : 
    6522      239719 :     iExponent = iExponent - (127 - 15);
    6523      239719 :     iMantissa = iMantissa >> 13;
    6524             : 
    6525             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6526             : /*       Assemble sign, exponent and mantissa.                          */
    6527             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6528             : 
    6529             :     // coverity[overflow_sink]
    6530      239719 :     return static_cast<GUInt16>((iSign << 15) | (iExponent << 10) | iMantissa);
    6531             : }
    6532             : 
    6533             : /************************************************************************/
    6534             : /*                            IWriteBlock()                             */
    6535             : /************************************************************************/
    6536             : 
    6537        5172 : CPLErr GTiffOddBitsBand::IWriteBlock( int nBlockXOff, int nBlockYOff,
    6538             :                                       void *pImage )
    6539             : 
    6540             : {
    6541        5172 :     m_poGDS->Crystalize();
    6542             : 
    6543        5172 :     if( m_poGDS->m_bWriteError )
    6544             :     {
    6545             :         // Report as an error if a previously loaded block couldn't be written
    6546             :         // correctly.
    6547           0 :         return CE_Failure;
    6548             :     }
    6549             : 
    6550       10344 :     CPLAssert( m_poGDS != nullptr
    6551             :                && nBlockXOff >= 0
    6552             :                && nBlockYOff >= 0
    6553        5172 :                && pImage != nullptr );
    6554             : 
    6555        5172 :     if( eDataType == GDT_Float32 && m_poGDS->m_nBitsPerSample != 16 )
    6556             :     {
    6557             :         CPLError(CE_Failure, CPLE_NotSupported,
    6558             :                  "Writing float data with nBitsPerSample = %d is unsupported",
    6559           0 :                  m_poGDS->m_nBitsPerSample);
    6560           0 :         return CE_Failure;
    6561             :     }
    6562             : 
    6563             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    6564             : /*      Load the block buffer.                                          */

    8802        3371 : void GTiffDataset::InitCompressionThreads( char** papszOptions )
    8803             : {

    8809        1278 :     if( pszValue == nullptr )
    8810        1250 :         pszValue = CPLGetConfigOption("GDAL_NUM_THREADS", nullptr);
    8811        1278 :     if( pszValue )
    8812             :     {
    8813             :         const int nThreads =
    8814          28 :             EQUAL(pszValue, "ALL_CPUS") ? CPLGetNumCPUs() : atoi(pszValue);
    8815          28 :         if( nThreads > 1 )
    8816             :         {
    8817          52 :             if( m_nCompression == COMPRESSION_NONE ||
    8818          25 :                 m_nCompression == COMPRESSION_JPEG )
    8819             :             {
    8820             :                 CPLDebug( "GTiff",
    8821           6 :                           "NUM_THREADS ignored with uncompressed or JPEG" );
    8822             :             }
    8823             :             else
    8824             :             {
    8825          21 :                 CPLDebug("GTiff", "Using %d threads for compression", nThreads);
    8826             :

    8828             :                 {

    8832             :                     {

    8834             :                     }
    8835             :                     else
    8836             :                     {

    8838             :                     }
    8839          21 :                     gpoCompressThreadPool = nullptr;
    8840             :                 }
    8841             : 
    8842          21 :                 if( m_poCompressThreadPool == nullptr )

    8846             :                     {
    8847           0 :                         delete m_poCompressThreadPool;
    8848           0 :                         m_poCompressThreadPool = nullptr;
    8849             :                     }
    8850             :                 }
    8851          21 :                 if( m_poCompressThreadPool != nullptr )
    8852             :                 {
    8853             :                     // Add a margin of an extra job w.r.t thread number
    8854             :                     // so as to optimize compression time (enables the main
    8855             :                     // thread to do boring I/O while all CPUs are working).
    8856          21 :                     m_asCompressionJobs.resize(nThreads + 1);

    8881             :                 }

    8891             :         }

   10023             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   10024             : /*      Cleanup overviews objects, and get offsets to all overview      */

   10032           1 :         delete m_papoOverviewDS[i];
   10033             :     }
   10034             : 
   10035             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   10036             : /*      Loop through all the directories, translating the offsets       */

   10039           2 :     std::vector<uint16> anOvDirIndexes;

   10041             : 
   10042           1 :     TIFFSetDirectory( m_hTIFF, 0 );

   10044             :     while( true )

   10053             :             }

   10061             :     }
   10062             : 
   10063             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   10064             : /*      Actually unlink the target directories.  Note that we do        */

   10067             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */

   10081             :

   11179             : /*                         AppendMetadataItem()                         */

   11233             : /************************************************************************/
   11234             : /*                         WriteMDMetadata()                            */
   11235             : /************************************************************************/
   11236             : 
   11237      201407 : static void WriteMDMetadata( GDALMultiDomainMetadata *poMDMD, TIFF *hTIFF,
   11238             :                              CPLXMLNode **ppsRoot, CPLXMLNode **ppsTail,
   11239             :                              int nBand, GTiffProfile eProfile )
   11240             : 
   11241             : {
   11242             : 
   11243             : /* ==================================================================== */
   11244             : /*      Process each domain.                                            */
   11245             : /* ==================================================================== */
   11246      201407 :     char **papszDomainList = poMDMD->GetDomainList();
   11247      204304 :     for( int iDomain = 0;
   11248        5289 :          papszDomainList && papszDomainList[iDomain];
   11249             :          ++iDomain )
   11250             :     {
   11251        2897 :         char **papszMD = poMDMD->GetMetadata( papszDomainList[iDomain] );
   11252        2897 :         bool bIsXML = false;
   11253             : 
   11254        2897 :         if( EQUAL(papszDomainList[iDomain], "IMAGE_STRUCTURE") )
   11255        1848 :             continue;  // Ignored.
   11256        1049 :         if( EQUAL(papszDomainList[iDomain], "COLOR_PROFILE") )
   11257           3 :             continue;  // Ignored.
   11258        1046 :         if( EQUAL(papszDomainList[iDomain], MD_DOMAIN_RPC) )
   11259           7 :             continue;  // Handled elsewhere.
   11260        2078 :         if( EQUAL(papszDomainList[iDomain], "xml:ESRI")
   11261        1039 :             && CPLTestBool(CPLGetConfigOption( "ESRI_XML_PAM", "NO" )) )
   11262           1 :             continue;  // Handled elsewhere.
   11263        1038 :         if( EQUAL(papszDomainList[iDomain], "xml:XMP") )
   11264           2 :             continue;  // Handled in SetMetadata.
   11265             : 
   11266        1036 :         if( STARTS_WITH_CI(papszDomainList[iDomain], "xml:") )
   11267           2 :             bIsXML = true;
   11268             : 
   11269             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   11270             : /*      Process each item in this domain.                               */
   11271             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   11272        4170 :         for( int iItem = 0; papszMD && papszMD[iItem]; ++iItem )
   11273             :         {
   11274        3134 :             const char *pszItemValue = nullptr;

   11276             : 
   11277        3134 :             if( bIsXML )
   11278             :             {

   11281             :             }

   11283             :             {

   11285        3132 :                 if( pszItemName == nullptr )
   11286             :                 {
   11287             :                     CPLDebug( "GTiff",
   11288           1 :                               "Invalid metadata item : %s", papszMD[iItem] );
   11289           1 :                     continue;
   11290             :                 }
   11291             :             }

   11765             : /************************************************************************/
   11766             : /*                         PushMetadataToPam()                          */
   11767             : /*                                                                      */
   11768             : /*      When producing a strict profile TIFF or if our aggregate        */
   11769             : /*      metadata is too big for a single tiff tag we may end up         */
   11770             : /*      needing to write it via the PAM mechanisms.  This method        */
   11771             : /*      copies all the appropriate metadata into the PAM level          */
   11772             : /*      metadata object but with special care to avoid copying          */
   11773             : /*      metadata handled in other ways in TIFF format.                  */
   11774             : /************************************************************************/
   11775             : 
   11776          48 : void GTiffDataset::PushMetadataToPam()
   11777             :

   11780          48 :         return;
   11781             : 
   11782             :     const bool bStardardColorInterp =
   11783          48 :         IsStandardColorInterpretation(this, m_nPhotometric, m_papszCreationOptions);
   11784             : 
   11785         160 :     for( int nBand = 0; nBand <= GetRasterCount(); ++nBand )
   11786             :     {
   11787         112 :         GDALMultiDomainMetadata *poSrcMDMD = nullptr;
   11788         112 :         GTiffRasterBand *poBand = nullptr;
   11789             : 
   11790         112 :         if( nBand == 0 )
   11791             :         {
   11792          48 :             poSrcMDMD = &(this->m_oGTiffMDMD);
   11793             :         }

   11798             :         }
   11799             : 
   11800             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */

   11809             : 
   11810         126 :             if( EQUAL(papszDomainList[iDomain],MD_DOMAIN_RPC)
   11811         125 :                 || EQUAL(papszDomainList[iDomain],MD_DOMAIN_IMD)

   11875          10 :     adfRPCTag[1] = -1.0;  // Error Random
   11876             : 
   11877          10 :     adfRPCTag[2] = sRPC.dfLINE_OFF;

   11890          10 :     memcpy( adfRPCTag + 52, sRPC.adfSAMP_NUM_COEFF, sizeof(double) * 20 );
   11891          10 :     memcpy( adfRPCTag + 72, sRPC.adfSAMP_DEN_COEFF, sizeof(double) * 20 );

   12039             : {
   12040             : 
   12041             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   12042             : /*      YCbCr JPEG compressed images should be translated on the fly    */
   12043             : /*      to RGB by libtiff/libjpeg unless specifically requested         */
   12044             : /*      otherwise.                                                      */
   12045             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */

   12047         168 :         && m_nPhotometric == PHOTOMETRIC_YCBCR
   12048       24546 :         && CPLTestBool( CPLGetConfigOption("CONVERT_YCBCR_TO_RGB",
   12049          96 :                                               "YES") ) )
   12050             :     {
   12051          96 :         int nColorMode = JPEGCOLORMODE_RAW;  // Initialize to 0;
   12052             : 
   12053          96 :         TIFFGetField( hTIFF, TIFFTAG_JPEGCOLORMODE, &nColorMode );
   12054          96 :         if( nColorMode != JPEGCOLORMODE_RGB )
   12055             :         {
   12056          96 :             TIFFSetField(hTIFF, TIFFTAG_JPEGCOLORMODE, JPEGCOLORMODE_RGB);
   12057             :         }
   12058             :     }
   12059             : 
   12060             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   12061             : /*      Propagate any quality settings.                                 */
   12062             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   12063       24450 :     if( GetAccess() == GA_Update )
   12064             :     {
   12065             :         // Now, reset zip and jpeg quality.
   12066       23904 :         if(m_nJpegQuality > 0 && m_nCompression == COMPRESSION_JPEG)
   12067             :         {
   12068             : #ifdef DEBUG_VERBOSE

   12070             :                       m_nJpegQuality );
   12071             : #endif
   12072          81 :             TIFFSetField(hTIFF, TIFFTAG_JPEGQUALITY, m_nJpegQuality);
   12073             :         }
   12074       23904 :         if(m_nJpegTablesMode >= 0 && m_nCompression == COMPRESSION_JPEG)

   12077           0 :                            m_nCompression == COMPRESSION_LERC) )

   12146             : 
   12147          14 : static bool GTIFFExtendMemoryFile( const CPLString& osTmpFilename,
   12148             :                                    VSILFILE* fpTemp,
   12149             :                                    VSILFILE* fpL,
   12150             :                                    int nNewLength,
   12151             :                                    GByte*& pabyBuffer,
   12152             :                                    vsi_l_offset& nDataLength )
   12153             : {
   12154          14 :     if( nNewLength <= static_cast<int>(nDataLength) )
   12155          10 :         return true;

   12157           0 :         return false;

   12159           4 :     if( VSIFWriteL(&ch, 1, 1, fpTemp) != 1 )
   12160           0 :         return false;

   12162             :     pabyBuffer = static_cast<GByte*>(
   12163           4 :         VSIGetMemFileBuffer( osTmpFilename, &nDataLength, FALSE) );
   12164           4 :     const int nToRead = nNewLength - nOldDataLength;
   12165             :     const int nRead = static_cast<int>(
   12166           4 :         VSIFReadL( pabyBuffer + nOldDataLength, 1, nToRead, fpL) );
   12167           4 :     if( nRead != nToRead )
   12168             :     {
   12169             :         CPLError(CE_Failure, CPLE_FileIO,
   12170           0 :                  "Needed to read %d bytes. Only %d got", nToRead, nRead);
   12171           0 :         return false;
   12172             :     }
   12173           4 :     return true;
   12174             : }

   12176             : /************************************************************************/
   12177             : /*                         GTIFFMakeBufferedStream()                    */
   12178             : /************************************************************************/
   12179             : 
   12180           9 : static bool GTIFFMakeBufferedStream(GDALOpenInfo* poOpenInfo)
   12181             : {
   12182           9 :     CPLString osTmpFilename;
   12183             :     static int nCounter = 0;
   12184           9 :     osTmpFilename.Printf("/vsimem/stream_%d.tif", ++nCounter);
   12185           9 :     VSILFILE* fpTemp = VSIFOpenL(osTmpFilename, "wb+");
   12186           9 :     if( fpTemp == nullptr )
   12187           0 :         return false;
   12188             :     // The seek is needed for /vsistdin/ that has some rewind capabilities.
   12189           9 :     if( VSIFSeekL(poOpenInfo->fpL, poOpenInfo->nHeaderBytes, SEEK_SET) != 0 )
   12190             :     {
   12191           0 :         CPL_IGNORE_RET_VAL(VSIFCloseL(fpTemp));
   12192           0 :         return false;
   12193             :     }
   12194           9 :     CPLAssert( static_cast<int>( VSIFTellL(poOpenInfo->fpL) ) ==
   12195           9 :                poOpenInfo->nHeaderBytes );
   12196           9 :     if( VSIFWriteL(poOpenInfo->pabyHeader, poOpenInfo->nHeaderBytes,
   12197           9 :                    1, fpTemp) != 1 )
   12198             :     {
   12199           0 :         CPL_IGNORE_RET_VAL(VSIFCloseL(fpTemp));
   12200           0 :         return false;
   12201             :     }
   12202           9 :     vsi_l_offset nDataLength = 0;
   12203             :     GByte* pabyBuffer =
   12204             :         static_cast<GByte*>(
   12205           9 :             VSIGetMemFileBuffer( osTmpFilename, &nDataLength, FALSE) );
   12206           9 :     const bool bLittleEndian = (pabyBuffer[0] == 'I');
   12207             : #if CPL_IS_LSB
   12208           9 :     const bool bSwap = !bLittleEndian;

   12210             :     const bool bSwap = bLittleEndian;
   12211             : #endif

   12213           9 :     vsi_l_offset nMaxOffset = 0;
   12214           9 :     if( bBigTIFF )
   12215             :     {
   12216           2 :         GUInt64 nTmp = 0;

   12676             : /************************************************************************/
   12677             : /*                         LoadMDAreaOrPoint()                          */
   12678             : /************************************************************************/
   12679             : 
   12680             : // This is a light version of LookForProjection(), which saves the

   12682             : // access to a raw GeoTIFF key, and not build the full projection object.

   12684        6679 : void GTiffDataset::LoadMDAreaOrPoint()

   12688       12886 :         return;
   12689             : 
   12690         472 :     m_bLookedForMDAreaOrPoint = true;
   12691             : 
   12692         472 :     GTIF* hGTIF = GTiffDatasetGTIFNew(m_hTIFF);
   12693             : 
   12694         472 :     if( !hGTIF )
   12695             :     {
   12696             :         CPLError( CE_Warning, CPLE_AppDefined,
   12697           0 :                   "GeoTIFF tags apparently corrupt, they are being ignored." );
   12698             :     }
   12699             :     else
   12700             :     {
   12701         472 :         GTiffDatasetSetAreaOrPointMD( hGTIF, m_oGTiffMDMD );

   12704             :     }

   12810             : /************************************************************************/
   12811             : /*                          AdjustLinearUnit()                          */
   12812             : /*                                                                      */
   12813             : /*      The following code is only used in ESRI Builds and there is     */
   12814             : /*      outstanding discussion on whether it is even appropriate        */
   12815             : /*      then.                                                           */
   12816             : /************************************************************************/
   12817             : #ifdef ESRI_BUILD
   12818             : 
   12819             : void GTiffDataset::AdjustLinearUnit( short UOMLength )

   12821             :     if( !pszProjection || strlen(pszProjection) == 0 )
   12822             :         return;
   12823             :     if( UOMLength == 9001 )
   12824             :     {
   12825             :         char* pstr = strstr(pszProjection, "PARAMETER");
   12826             :         if( !pstr )
   12827             :             return;
   12828             :         pstr = strstr(pstr, "UNIT[");
   12829             :         if( !pstr )
   12830             :             return;
   12831             :         pstr = strchr(pstr, ',') + 1;
   12832             :         if( !pstr )
   12833             :             return;
   12834             :         char* pstr1 = strchr(pstr, ']');
   12835             :         if( !pstr1 || pstr1 - pstr >= 128 )
   12836             :             return;
   12837             :         char csUnitStr[128];
   12838             :         strncpy(csUnitStr, pstr, pstr1 - pstr);
   12839             :         csUnitStr[pstr1-pstr] = '\0';
   12840             :         const double csUnit = CPLAtof(csUnitStr);
   12841             :         if( fabs(csUnit - 1.0) > 0.000001 )
   12842             :         {
   12843             :             for( long i = 0; i < 6; ++i )
   12844             :                 adfGeoTransform[i] /= csUnit;
   12845             :         }
   12846             :     }
   12847             : }
   12848             : 
   12849             : #endif  // def ESRI_BUILD

   13207        5355 :                                     static_cast<uint32>(pTable[i]));
   13208             :     }
   13209             : 
   13210          21 :     return sValue;
   13211             : }

   14949             : /*                          ScanDirectories()                           */

   14954             : 
   14955      670305 : void GTiffDataset::ScanDirectories()
   14956             :

   16080             :     // This trick
   16081             :     // creates a temporary in-memory file and fetches its JPEG tables so that
   16082             :     // we can directly set them, before tif_jpeg.c compute them at the first
   16083             :     // strip/tile writing, which is too late, since we have already crystalized
   16084             :     // the directory. This way we avoid a directory rewriting.

   16086       11795 :         !STARTS_WITH(pszFilename, szJPEGGTiffDatasetTmpPrefix) &&
   16087             :         CPLTestBool(
   16088        3178 :             CSLFetchNameValueDef(papszParmList, "WRITE_JPEGTABLE_TAG", "YES")) )
   16089             :     {
   16090             :         GTiffWriteJPEGTables( l_hTIFF,
   16091             :                               CSLFetchNameValue(papszParmList, "PHOTOMETRIC"),
   16092             :                               CSLFetchNameValue(papszParmList, "JPEG_QUALITY"),
   16093             :                               CSLFetchNameValue(papszParmList,
   16094        3178 :                                                 "JPEGTABLESMODE") );
   16095             :     }
   16096             : 
   16097        8617 :     *pfpL = l_fpL;
   16098             : 
   16099        8617 :     return l_hTIFF;

   16102             : /************************************************************************/
   16103             : /*                      GTiffWriteJPEGTables()                          */
   16104             : /*                                                                      */
   16105             : /*      Sets the TIFFTAG_JPEGTABLES (and TIFFTAG_REFERENCEBLACKWHITE)   */
   16106             : /*      tags immediately, instead of relying on the TIFF JPEG codec     */
   16107             : /*      to write them when it starts compressing imagery. This avoids   */
   16108             : /*      an IFD rewrite at the end of the file.                          */
   16109             : /*      Must be used after having set TIFFTAG_SAMPLESPERPIXEL,          */
   16110             : /*      TIFFTAG_BITSPERSAMPLE.                                          */
   16111             : /************************************************************************/
   16112             : 
   16113        3219 : void GTiffWriteJPEGTables( TIFF* hTIFF,
   16114             :                            const char* pszPhotometric,
   16115             :                            const char* pszJPEGQuality,
   16116             :                            const char* pszJPEGTablesMode )
   16117             : {
   16118             :     // This trick
   16119             :     // creates a temporary in-memory file and fetches its JPEG tables so that
   16120             :     // we can directly set them, before tif_jpeg.c compute them at the first
   16121             :     // strip/tile writing, which is too late, since we have already crystalized
   16122             :     // the directory. This way we avoid a directory rewriting.
   16123        3219 :     uint16 nBands = 0;
   16124        3219 :     if( !TIFFGetField( hTIFF, TIFFTAG_SAMPLESPERPIXEL,
   16125        3219 :                         &nBands ) )
   16126           0 :         nBands = 1;
   16127             : 
   16128        3219 :     uint16 l_nBitsPerSample = 0;
   16129        3219 :     if( !TIFFGetField(hTIFF, TIFFTAG_BITSPERSAMPLE,
   16130        3219 :                         &(l_nBitsPerSample)) )
   16131           0 :         l_nBitsPerSample = 1;
   16132             : 
   16133        3219 :     CPLString osTmpFilenameIn;
   16134        3219 :     osTmpFilenameIn.Printf("%s%p", szJPEGGTiffDatasetTmpPrefix, hTIFF);
   16135        3219 :     VSILFILE* fpTmp = nullptr;
   16136        6438 :     CPLString osTmp;
   16137        3219 :     char** papszLocalParameters = nullptr;
   16138        3219 :     const int nInMemImageWidth = 16;
   16139        3219 :     const int nInMemImageHeight = 16;
   16140             :     papszLocalParameters = CSLSetNameValue( papszLocalParameters,
   16141        3219 :                                             "COMPRESS", "JPEG" );

   16143             :                                             "JPEG_QUALITY",
   16144        3219 :                                             pszJPEGQuality );

   16147             :     papszLocalParameters = CSLSetNameValue( papszLocalParameters,

   16150        3219 :                                           );

   16153        3219 :                                             CPLSPrintf("%u", l_nBitsPerSample));

   16156        3219 :                                             pszJPEGTablesMode );
   16157             : 
   16158             :     TIFF* hTIFFTmp = GTiffDataset::CreateLL(
   16159             :                     osTmpFilenameIn, nInMemImageWidth, nInMemImageHeight,
   16160        3219 :                     (nBands <= 4) ? nBands : 1,

   16162        6438 :                     papszLocalParameters, &fpTmp, osTmp );
   16163        3219 :     CSLDestroy(papszLocalParameters);

   16172        3219 :         TIFFWriteDirectory( hTIFFTmp );
   16173        3219 :         TIFFSetDirectory( hTIFFTmp, 0 );
   16174             :         // Now, reset quality and jpegcolormode.

   16214             : /*                            GuessJPEGQuality()                        */

   16216             : /*      Guess JPEG quality from JPEGTABLES tag.                         */
   16217             : /************************************************************************/
   16218             : 
   16219        6798 : static const GByte* GTIFFFindNextTable( const GByte* paby, GByte byMarker,
   16220             :                                         int nLen, int* pnLenTable )
   16221             : {
   16222       20253 :     for( int i = 0; i + 1 < nLen; )
   16223             :     {
   16224       13455 :         if( paby[i] != 0xFF )
   16225           0 :             return nullptr;
   16226       13455 :         ++i;
   16227       13455 :         if( paby[i] == 0xD8 )
   16228             :         {
   16229        6422 :             ++i;
   16230        6422 :             continue;
   16231             :         }
   16232        7033 :         if( i + 2 >= nLen )
   16233         340 :             return nullptr;
   16234        6693 :         int nMarkerLen = paby[i+1] * 256 + paby[i+2];
   16235        6693 :         if( i+1+nMarkerLen >= nLen )
   16236           0 :             return nullptr;
   16237        6693 :         if( paby[i] == byMarker )
   16238             :         {
   16239        6458 :             if( pnLenTable ) *pnLenTable = nMarkerLen;
   16240        6458 :             return paby + i + 1;
   16241             :         }
   16242         235 :         i += 1 + nMarkerLen;
   16243             :     }
   16244           0 :     return nullptr;
   16245             : }
   16246             : 
   16247             : // We assume that if there are several quantization tables, they are
   16248             : // in the same order. Which is a reasonable assumption for updating
   16249             : // a file generated by ourselves.
   16250        3090 : static bool GTIFFQuantizationTablesEqual( const GByte* paby1, int nLen1,
   16251             :                                           const GByte* paby2, int nLen2 )
   16252             : {
   16253        3090 :     bool bFound = false;
   16254             :     while( true )
   16255             :     {
   16256        3278 :         int nLenTable1 = 0;
   16257        3278 :         int nLenTable2 = 0;
   16258             :         const GByte* paby1New =
   16259        3278 :             GTIFFFindNextTable(paby1, 0xDB, nLen1, &nLenTable1);
   16260             :         const GByte* paby2New =
   16261        3278 :             GTIFFFindNextTable(paby2, 0xDB, nLen2, &nLenTable2);
   16262        3278 :         if( paby1New == nullptr && paby2New == nullptr )
   16263        3208 :             return bFound;
   16264        3160 :         if( paby1New == nullptr || paby2New == nullptr )
   16265           0 :             return false;
   16266        3160 :         if( nLenTable1 != nLenTable2 )
   16267        1150 :             return false;
   16268        2010 :         if( memcmp(paby1New, paby2New, nLenTable1) != 0 )
   16269        1822 :             return false;
   16270         188 :         paby1New += nLenTable1;
   16271         188 :         paby2New += nLenTable2;
   16272         188 :         nLen1 -= static_cast<int>(paby1New - paby1);
   16273         188 :         nLen2 -= static_cast<int>(paby2New - paby2);
   16274         188 :         paby1 = paby1New;
   16275         188 :         paby2 = paby2New;
   16276         188 :         bFound = true;
   16277         188 :     }
   16278             : }
   16279             : 
   16280         125 : int GTiffDataset::GuessJPEGQuality( bool& bOutHasQuantizationTable,
   16281             :                                     bool& bOutHasHuffmanTable )
   16282             : {
   16283         125 :     CPLAssert( m_nCompression == COMPRESSION_JPEG );
   16284         125 :     uint32 nJPEGTableSize = 0;
   16285         125 :     void* pJPEGTable = nullptr;
   16286         125 :     if( !TIFFGetField(m_hTIFF, TIFFTAG_JPEGTABLES, &nJPEGTableSize, &pJPEGTable) )
   16287             :     {
   16288           4 :         bOutHasQuantizationTable = false;
   16289           4 :         bOutHasHuffmanTable = false;
   16290           4 :         return -1;

   17106             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   17107             : /*      Should we use optimized way of copying from an input JPEG       */

   17109             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   17110             : 
   17111             : // TODO(schwehr): Refactor bDirectCopyFromJPEG to be a const.

   17113         638 :     bool bDirectCopyFromJPEG = false;
   17114             : #endif
   17115             :

   17118             : #ifdef JPEG_DIRECT_COPY

   17120             :         GTIFF_CanDirectCopyFromJPEG(poSrcDS, papszCreateOptions) )

   17122             :         CPLDebug("GTiff", "Using special direct copy mode from a JPEG dataset");

   17125             :     }

   17127             : 
   17128             : #ifdef HAVE_LIBJPEG
   17129         638 :     bool bCopyFromJPEG = false;
   17130             : 
   17131             :     // When CreateCopy'ing() from a JPEG dataset, and asking for COMPRESS=JPEG,

   17133             :     // strip/tile dimensions, specifying JPEG_QUALITY option, incompatible
   17134             :     // PHOTOMETRIC with the source colorspace, etc.) to avoid the lossy steps

   17136        1276 :     if( !bDirectCopyFromJPEG &&
   17137         638 :         GTIFF_CanCopyFromJPEG(poSrcDS, papszCreateOptions) )

   17140             : 
   17141          12 :         bCopyFromJPEG = true;
   17142             :     }
   17143             : #endif

   17147             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */

   19229             : /************************************************************************/
   19230             : /*                       PrepareTIFFErrorFormat()                       */
   19231             : /*                                                                      */
   19232             : /*      sometimes the "module" has stuff in it that has special         */
   19233             : /*      meaning in a printf() style format, so we try to escape it.     */
   19234             : /*      For now we hope the only thing we have to escape is %'s.        */
   19235             : /************************************************************************/
   19236             : 
   19237        1519 : static char *PrepareTIFFErrorFormat( const char *module, const char *fmt )
   19238             : 
   19239             : {
   19240        1519 :     const size_t nModuleSize = strlen(module);
   19241        1519 :     const size_t nModFmtSize = nModuleSize * 2 + strlen(fmt) + 2;
   19242        1519 :     char *pszModFmt = static_cast<char *>( CPLMalloc( nModFmtSize ) );
   19243             : 
   19244        1519 :     size_t iOut = 0;  // Used after for.
   19245             : 
   19246       15229 :     for( size_t iIn = 0; iIn < nModuleSize; ++iIn )
   19247             :     {
   19248       13710 :         if( module[iIn] == '%' )
   19249             :         {
   19250           0 :             CPLAssert(iOut < nModFmtSize - 2);
   19251           0 :             pszModFmt[iOut++] = '%';
   19252           0 :             pszModFmt[iOut++] = '%';
   19253             :         }
   19254             :         else
   19255             :         {
   19256       13710 :             CPLAssert(iOut < nModFmtSize - 1);
   19257       13710 :             pszModFmt[iOut++] = module[iIn];
   19258             :         }
   19259             :     }
   19260        1519 :     CPLAssert(iOut < nModFmtSize);
   19261        1519 :     pszModFmt[iOut] = '\0';
   19262        1519 :     strcat( pszModFmt, ":" );
   19263        1519 :     strcat( pszModFmt, fmt );
   19264             : 
   19265        1519 :     return pszModFmt;
   19266             : }
   19267             : 
   19268             : /************************************************************************/
   19269             : /*                        GTiffWarningHandler()                         */
   19270             : /************************************************************************/
   19271             : static void
   19272        1406 : GTiffWarningHandler(const char* module, const char* fmt, va_list ap )
   19273             : {
   19274        1406 :     if( strstr(fmt,"nknown field") != nullptr )
   19275        1411 :         return;
   19276             : 
   19277        1401 :     char *pszModFmt = PrepareTIFFErrorFormat( module, fmt );
   19278        1401 :     if( strstr(fmt, "does not end in null byte") != nullptr )
   19279             :     {
   19280           1 :         CPLString osMsg;
   19281           1 :         osMsg.vPrintf(pszModFmt, ap);
   19282           1 :         CPLDebug( "GTiff", "%s", osMsg.c_str() );
   19283             :     }
   19284             :     else
   19285             :     {
   19286        1400 :         CPLErrorV( CE_Warning, CPLE_AppDefined, pszModFmt, ap );
   19287             :     }
   19288        1401 :     CPLFree( pszModFmt );
   19289             : }

   19291             : /************************************************************************/
   19292             : /*                         GTiffErrorHandler()                          */
   19293             : /************************************************************************/
   19294             : static void
   19295         118 : GTiffErrorHandler( const char* module, const char* fmt, va_list ap )
   19296             : {
   19297         118 :     if( strcmp(fmt, "Maximum TIFF file size exceeded") == 0 )
   19298             :     {
   19299             :         // Ideally there would be a thread-safe way of setting this flag,
   19300             :         // but we cannot really use the extended error handler, since the
   19301             :         // handler is for all TIFF handles, and not necessarily the ones of
   19302             :         // this driver.
   19303           0 :         if( bGlobalInExternalOvr )
   19304             :             fmt =
   19305             :                 "Maximum TIFF file size exceeded. "
   19306           0 :                 "Use --config BIGTIFF_OVERVIEW YES configuration option.";
   19307             :         else
   19308             :             fmt =
   19309             :                 "Maximum TIFF file size exceeded. "
   19310           0 :                 "Use BIGTIFF=YES creation option.";
   19311             :     }
   19312             : 
   19313         118 :     char* pszModFmt = PrepareTIFFErrorFormat( module, fmt );
   19314         118 :     CPLErrorV( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined, pszModFmt, ap );
   19315         118 :     CPLFree( pszModFmt );
   19316         118 : }

   19333             :           TRUE, FALSE, const_cast<char*>( "GDALNoDataValue" ) },

   19336             :         { TIFFTAG_TIFF_RSID, -1, -1, TIFF_ASCII, FIELD_CUSTOM,

   19349             : /************************************************************************/
   19350             : /*                          GTiffOneTimeInit()                          */
   19351             : /*                                                                      */
   19352             : /*      This is stuff that is initialized for the TIFF library just     */
   19353             : /*      once.  We deliberately defer the initialization till the        */
   19354             : /*      first time we are likely to call into libtiff to avoid          */
   19355             : /*      unnecessary paging in of the library for GDAL apps that         */
   19356             : /*      don't use it.                                                   */
   19357             : /************************************************************************/
   19358             : #if defined(HAVE_DLFCN_H) && !defined(WIN32)
   19359             : #include <dlfcn.h>
   19360             : #endif
   19361             : 
   19362             : static std::mutex oDeleteMutex;
   19363             : #ifdef HAVE_LERC
   19364             : static TIFFCodec* pLercCodec = nullptr;
   19365             : #endif
   19366             : 
   19367       20937 : int GTiffOneTimeInit()
   19368             : 
   19369             : {
   19370       20937 :     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> oLock(oDeleteMutex);
   19371             :  
   19372             : #ifdef HAVE_LERC
   19373       21000 :     if( pLercCodec == nullptr )
   19374             :     {
   19375         400 :         pLercCodec = TIFFRegisterCODEC(COMPRESSION_LERC, "LERC", TIFFInitLERC);
   19376             :     }
   19377             : #endif
   19378             : 
   19379             :     static bool bOneTimeInitDone = false;
   19380       21000 :     if( bOneTimeInitDone )
   19381       20600 :         return TRUE;
   19382             : 
   19383         400 :     bOneTimeInitDone = true;
   19384             : 
   19385             :     // This is a frequent configuration error that is difficult to track down
   19386             :     // for people unaware of the issue : GDAL built against internal libtiff
   19387             :     // (4.X), but used by an application that links with external libtiff (3.X)
   19388             :     // Note: on my conf, the order that cause GDAL to crash - and that is
   19389             :     // detected by the following code - is "-ltiff -lgdal". "-lgdal -ltiff"
   19390             :     // works for the GTiff driver but probably breaks the application that
   19391             :     // believes it uses libtiff 3.X but we cannot detect that.
   19392             : #if !defined(RENAME_INTERNAL_LIBTIFF_SYMBOLS)
   19393             : #if defined(HAVE_DLFCN_H) && !defined(WIN32)
   19394             :     const char* (*pfnVersion)(void);
   19395             : #ifdef HAVE_GCC_WARNING_ZERO_AS_NULL_POINTER_CONSTANT
   19396             : #pragma GCC diagnostic push
   19397             : #pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wzero-as-null-pointer-constant"
   19398             : #endif
   19399             :     pfnVersion = reinterpret_cast<const char* (*)(void)>(dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT, "TIFFGetVersion"));
   19400             : #ifdef HAVE_GCC_WARNING_ZERO_AS_NULL_POINTER_CONSTANT
   19401             : #pragma GCC diagnostic pop
   19402             : #endif
   19403             :     if( pfnVersion )
   19404             :     {
   19405             :         const char* pszVersion = pfnVersion();
   19406             :         if( pszVersion && strstr(pszVersion, "Version 3.") != nullptr )
   19407             :         {
   19408             :             CPLError(
   19409             :                 CE_Warning, CPLE_AppDefined,
   19410             :                 "libtiff version mismatch: You're linking against libtiff 3.X, "
   19411             :                 "but GDAL has been compiled against libtiff >= 4.0.0" );
   19412             :         }
   19413             :     }
   19414             : #endif  // HAVE_DLFCN_H
   19415             : #endif // !defined(RENAME_INTERNAL_LIBTIFF_SYMBOLS)
   19416             : 
   19417         400 :     _ParentExtender = TIFFSetTagExtender(GTiffTagExtender);
   19418             : 
   19419         400 :     TIFFSetWarningHandler( GTiffWarningHandler );
   19420         400 :     TIFFSetErrorHandler( GTiffErrorHandler );
   19421             : 
   19422         400 :     LibgeotiffOneTimeInit();
   19423             : 
   19424         400 :     return TRUE;
   19425             : }
   19426             :

   19445             : /************************************************************************/
   19446             : /*                   GTIFFGetCompressionMethod()                        */
   19447             : /************************************************************************/
   19448             : 
   19449        6695 : int GTIFFGetCompressionMethod(const char* pszValue, const char* pszVariableName)
   19450             : {
   19451        6695 :     int nCompression = COMPRESSION_NONE;
   19452        6695 :     if( EQUAL( pszValue, "NONE" ) )
   19453          19 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_NONE;
   19454        6676 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "JPEG" ) )
   19455        6407 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_JPEG;
   19456         269 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "LZW" ) )
   19457         113 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_LZW;
   19458         156 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "PACKBITS" ))
   19459           2 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_PACKBITS;
   19460         154 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "DEFLATE" ) || EQUAL( pszValue, "ZIP" ))
   19461          82 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_ADOBE_DEFLATE;
   19462          72 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "FAX3" )
   19463          72 :              || EQUAL( pszValue, "CCITTFAX3" ))
   19464           1 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_CCITTFAX3;
   19465          71 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "FAX4" )
   19466          71 :              || EQUAL( pszValue, "CCITTFAX4" ))
   19467          16 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_CCITTFAX4;
   19468          55 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "CCITTRLE" ) )
   19469           1 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_CCITTRLE;
   19470          54 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "LZMA" ) )
   19471          10 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_LZMA;
   19472          44 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "ZSTD" ) )
   19473          11 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_ZSTD;
   19474             : #ifdef HAVE_LERC
   19475          52 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "LERC" ) ||
   19476          35 :              EQUAL( pszValue, "LERC_DEFLATE" ) ||
   19477          16 :              EQUAL( pszValue, "LERC_ZSTD" ) )
   19478             :     {
   19479          19 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_LERC;
   19480             :     }
   19481             : #endif
   19482          14 :     else if( EQUAL( pszValue, "WEBP" ) )
   19483          12 :         nCompression = COMPRESSION_WEBP;
   19484             :     else
   19485             :         CPLError( CE_Warning, CPLE_IllegalArg,
   19486             :                   "%s=%s value not recognised, ignoring.",
   19487           2 :                   pszVariableName,pszValue );
   19488             : 
   19489       13369 :     if( nCompression != COMPRESSION_NONE &&
   19490        6674 :         !TIFFIsCODECConfigured(static_cast<uint16>(nCompression)) )
   19491             :     {
   19492             :         CPLError(
   19493             :             CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
   19494           0 :             "Cannot create TIFF file due to missing codec for %s.", pszValue );
   19495           0 :         return -1;
   19496             :     }
   19497             : 
   19498        6695 :     return nCompression;
   19499             : }
   19500             : 
   19501             : /************************************************************************/
   19502             : /*                     GTiffGetCompressValues()                         */
   19503             : /************************************************************************/
   19504             : 
   19505        1974 : CPLString GTiffGetCompressValues(bool& bHasLZW,
   19506             :                                  bool& bHasDEFLATE,
   19507             :                                  bool& bHasLZMA,
   19508             :                                  bool& bHasZSTD,
   19509             :                                  bool& bHasJPEG,
   19510             :                                  bool& bHasWebP,
   19511             :                                  bool bForCOG)
   19512             : {
   19513             : 
   19514             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   19515             : /*      Determine which compression codecs are available that we        */
   19516             : /*      want to advertise.  If we are using an old libtiff we won't     */
   19517             : /*      be able to find out so we just assume all are available.        */
   19518             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
   19519        1974 :     CPLString osCompressValues = "       <Value>NONE</Value>";
   19520             : 
   19521        1974 :     TIFFCodec *codecs = TIFFGetConfiguredCODECs();
   19522             : 
   19523       39480 :     for( TIFFCodec *c = codecs; c->name; ++c )
   19524             :     {
   19525       37506 :         if( c->scheme == COMPRESSION_PACKBITS && !bForCOG )
   19526             :         {
   19527         987 :             osCompressValues +=
   19528         987 :                     "       <Value>PACKBITS</Value>";
   19529             :         }
   19530       36519 :         else if( c->scheme == COMPRESSION_JPEG )
   19531             :         {
   19532        1974 :             bHasJPEG = true;
   19533        1974 :             osCompressValues +=
   19534        1974 :                     "       <Value>JPEG</Value>";
   19535             :         }
